cards.back: A CPU architecture where data and instructions are stored in the same main memory.

cards.front: Name three factors that affect CPU performance.
cards.back: Cache size, clock speed and number of cores.

cards.front: What is the difference between RISC and CISC processors?
cards.back: RISC uses a smaller, simpler instruction set, while CISC uses a larger, more complex instruction set.
RAM is volatile working memory that stores current programs and data.

cards.front: Give one example of an embedded system.
cards.back: Examples include washing machines, cars and traffic lights.
